'''Paddy Flynn''' (born 11 December 1987) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|wg}}er. He played for the [[Widnes Vikings]], and on loan from [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] at the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]]. Flynn also played for the [[Sheffield Eagles]] and the [[Rochdale Hornets]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org">{{cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/Paddy-Flynn/summary.html|title=Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org|publisher=rugbyleagueproject.org|date=31 December 2011|accessdate=1 January 2012}}</ref>

Flynn has appeared Outside of sport, Flynn also works part-time as a [[plumber]]. His hobbies include playing Clash of Clans, where he represents Yorkshire based CGDL.

==Background==
Flynn was born in [[St Helens, Merseyside]], England.

==Club career==
Flynn made his début for Widnes, scoring two [[Try (rugby)|tries]] against [[Barrow Raiders|Barrow]] at [[Craven Park (Barrow)|Craven Park]] on Sunday 16 March 2008.<ref name="Profile at widnesvikings.co.uk">{{cite web|url=http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/club/teams/player_profile?511846-paddy-flynn|title=Profile at widnesvikings.co.uk|publisher=widnesvikings.co.uk|date=31 December 2011|accessdate=1 January 2012}}</ref> Flynn scored his 100th Widnes try against the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] on 12 September 2015.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2015/sep/12/wakefield-trinity-wildcats-widnes-super-league-8s-match-report |title=Rhys Hanbury inspires Widnes to victory over Wakefield with four tries |newspaper=The Guardian |date=12 September 2015 |access-date=19 July 2024}}</ref>

In May 2016 Paddy joined the [[Castleford Tigers]] on loan for the rest of the 2016 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/13995/10268277/widnes-winger-paddy-flynn-joins-castleford-on-loan |title=Widnes winger Paddy Flynn joins Castleford on loan |website=Sky Sports |date=3 May 2016 |access-date=19 July 2024}}</ref>

At the beginning of May 2017, [[RFL Championship|Championship]] side the [[Sheffield Eagles]] signed Flynn until the end of the season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/flynn-flies-eagles |title=Flynn flies into the Eagles |website=Love Rugby League |date=5 May 2017 |access-date=19 July 2024}}</ref> He scored a try on his first appearance in the Championship for the Eagles in a 45-20 defeat at the hands of French side [[Toulouse Olympique]].

In November 2018, Flynn signed for [[Rochdale Hornets]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/rochdale-capture-paddy-flynn |title=Rochdale capture Paddy Flynn |website=Love Rugby League |date=5 November 2018 |access-date=19 July 2024}}</ref>
